steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The task is to contrast two chemical compounds: dichloromethane (an HCC, containing hydrogen, chlorine, and carbon) and tetradecafluorohexane (a PFC, containing fluorine and carbon). The answer must highlight what's different between them — specifically their elemental compositions.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Dichloromethane, a hydrochlorocarbon, is composed of hydrogen, chlorine, and carbon; tetradecafluorohexane, a perfluorocarbon, is composed of fluorine and carbon.: Lays out each compound's elements side by side, making the elemental difference immediately visible. ✓
  • Choice B — Dichloromethane and tetradecafluorohexane are chemical compounds used in cooling technologies; the compounds are known as refrigerants.: Emphasizes the compounds' shared category/function, not a contrast. ✗
  • Choice C — The hydrochlorocarbon dichloromethane and the perfluorocarbon tetradecafluorohexane are both refrigerants that can be used in cooling technologies like air conditioners.: Identifies different compound classes but emphasizes shared refrigerant use, not the elemental contrast. ✗
  • Choice D — Tetradecafluorohexane, a refrigerant, can be used in cooling technologies to absorb heat and release cold air.: Shifts away from elemental makeup, so the elemental contrast is lost. ✗
A is correct.
